records.ety: [NL., fr. Gr. <?/ smoke, cloud, stupor arising from fever; akin to <?/ to smoke, Skr. dh<?/pa smoke.]

defs: A contagious continued fever lasting from two to three weeks, attended with great prostration and cerebral disorder, and marked by a copious eruption of red spots upon the body. Also called jail fever, famine fever, putrid fever, spottled fever, etc. See Jail fever, under Jail.
